checks.a_analysis: The output content was compared against the source PDF for each section. All paragraphs and sentences in the output are present in the source document. No fabricated sections, introductory text, or concluding remarks were added by the conversion process.

The source document contains one URL on page 5. While this URL is missing in the output (see C6), no new or fabricated URLs, phone numbers, email addresses, or mailing addresses were introduced into the JSON.
All seven pages of the source PDF have been successfully converted. All primary sections (Minutes, Present, Proceedings, Reports, Committee Reports, News, etc.) are represented in the JSON structure.
The source document consists entirely of text paragraphs and headings; it does not contain any tables. Therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While there is a specific omission of a URL on page 5, the overall text content within that section (and all other sections) remains largely intact. The omission of the URL does not exceed the 25% threshold of a section's text required for a MAJOR fail under this specific check.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: A URL mentioned on page 5 of the source document is omitted in the conversion result. The source text contains the URL 'http://gisdata.usgs.net/website/NC3/viewer.htm' within the first paragraph of the Mecklenburg County section. In the output JSON, the text is split across reading_order elements 40 and 42, and the URL itself is completely missing, resulting in the text 'found at ), which is'. This is a minor content omission that alters the information available to the reader.
checks.b_evidence_source: They demonstrated a prototype National Map viewer (found at http://gisdata.usgs.net/website/NC3/viewer.htm), which is served out of the USGS EROS facility...
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "USGS. They demonstrated a prototype National Map viewer (found at" (element 40) ... "text": "), which is served out of the USGS EROS facility..." (element 42)
checks.b_evidence_location: Presentation: Mecklenburg County and the National Map section, Page 5

checks.a_analysis: The output was reviewed for repeated text blocks or sections that appear multiple times in the JSON compared to once in the source PDF. No content duplication was found. The conversion correctly handled page headers and footers by excluding them from the main structural content.

There are multiple failures in heading hierarchy and definition. First, 'Priority #2—Data Inventory' (element 28) is assigned level 2, whereas 'Priority #1', '#3', and '#4' are assigned level 3, creating an inconsistent hierarchy for parallel sections. Second, element 52 ('Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee (SMAC)') incorrectly swallows the entire subsequent paragraph text into the heading level 3 element, whereas other committee reports in the previous section were correctly identified as paragraphs with bolded starters.
checks.b_evidence_source: Priority #2—Data Inventory (Page 3); Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee (SMAC). Bill Holman said the next meeting... (Page 6)
checks.b_evidence_output: {"type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "Priority #2—Data Inventory"} (Element 28); "text": "Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee (SMAC). Bill Holman said the next meeting of a reconstituted SMAC has been scheduled for April 23rd." (Element 52)

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains no tables. This check is not applicable.

The reading order in the JSON output correctly follows the logical flow of the source document, including transitions across page boundaries. Text continues logically from the end of one page to the beginning of the next.

The document contains several 'ACTION' items. The conversion is structurally inconsistent: Actions #1 through #4 are converted into list structures (elements 13, 25, 27), while Actions #5 and #6 are converted into regular paragraphs (elements 59, 62). This breaks the structural pattern established in the first half of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: ACTION #5: Risk/benefit evaluation discussion... (Page 6); ACTION #6: CGIA staff to investigate... (Page 7)

checks.a_analysis: A critical government URL present in the source PDF on page 5 is completely missing from the JSON text content. The text ends at the opening parenthesis and resumes after the closing parenthesis, resulting in the loss of important structural and informational content.
checks.b_evidence_output: USGS. They demonstrated a prototype National Map viewer (found at (Element 40) ... ), which is served out of the USGS EROS facility (Element 42)

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the single-column visual organization of the source PDF. The sequence of sections, subsections, and action items matches the original document's intent. A table of contents has been added for navigation, which is an expected and acceptable difference for accessible HTML. The information flow is preserved.
There are no tables present in the source PDF or the rendered HTML output.

There are no meaningful images (such as logos, charts, or photos) in the source PDF. The document consists entirely of text and decorative formatting elements like horizontal lines, which are intentionally omitted in the HTML for accessibility.

There is no visual duplication of body content. While section headings appear in both the 'Contents' sidebar and the main body of the document, this is an intentional and acceptable design pattern for accessibility. The core text of paragraphs and action items appears only once in the document flow.
